title: T-021 — API: Cost history endpoints (POST/GET /api/technical/items/:code/cost)
prompt:
# T-021 — API: Cost history endpoints (POST/GET /api/technical/items/:code/cost)

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §11.1, §11.2, §11.6
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Cost POST with V-TEC-50..53 + history close + denormalized current cost.

ISO 4217 list lives in lib/shared/iso4217.ts (create if absent). Approver requirement only fires when source ∈ {manual,supplier_update}; d365_sync and variance_roll bypass V-TEC-53. Audit log entry on every write.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- app/api/technical/items/[item_code]/cost/route.ts [create]
- lib/technical/cost/service.ts [create]
- lib/shared/iso4217.ts [create]
- tests/api/cost.test.ts [create]

## Out of scope
- Cost roll-up (10-FINANCE)
- D365 cost diff UI

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given current cost 10.0, when POST cost_per_kg=12.5 source='manual' without approver header, then 422 V-TEC-53 (>20% delta).
2. Given POST cost_per_kg=-1, when validated, then 422 V-TEC-50.
3. Given valid POST, when committed, then prior history row.effective_to = new.effective_from - 1 day, and items.cost_per_kg updated.
4. Given GET, when called, then rows ordered by effective_from DESC.

## Test strategy
- RED tests across V-TEC-50/51/52/53 paths.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/api/cost.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not insert negative cost.
- Do not skip audit log.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
